summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Digital ID and payments turn to your face
articles.description: As mobile devices and government regulators embrace facial recognition technology, your face is becoming both wallet and identification.

articles.title: The B2B payments infrastructure gap: Most platforms weren’t built for agentic AI
articles.description: Legacy B2B tech stacks were designed to be compatible with automation — not today’s agentic AI.

articles.title: Simplifying the operations behind modern payments
articles.description: Reduce “operational drag” with a payment solution built for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source.

articles.title: Adyen promotes executives
articles.description: The Dutch payments processor appointed a chief product officer and an interim chief financial officer on Wednesday as it angles to integrate recent acquisitions.

articles.title: Nuvei names new C-suite executives
articles.description: The Canadian company that just bought New York-based Payoneer remade its C-suite, and is doubling down on agentic commerce.

articles.title: Amex, Apple team on rewards
articles.description: American Express card holders can now redeem their rewards at checkout via Apple Pay online, or in the app.

articles.title: Ramp elevates CTO to co-CEO
articles.description: “If you know us, this won’t feel like a change,” wrote Eric Glyman of fellow co-founder Karim Atiyeh’s move to the top of the fintech.

articles.title: Card networks, banks, fintechs partner on ‘low-cost’ stablecoin
articles.description: Visa, Mastercard, Klarna and MoneyGram were among the companies that lent their backing to a digital asset launched Tuesday by Open Standard.
